reasoning: Peer set uses predominantly cool/neutral tones (dark steel, silver-gray helmets, muted gold staff) with restrained saturation. Candidate fire sword is aggressively warm — bright orange-red blade with near-white yellow highlights that blow out far beyond the intensity of any peer. (1) OUTLINE WEIGHT: 1px consistent, matches peers — OK. (2) OUTLINE COLOR: dark outlines present — OK. (3) SHADOW DIRECTION: peers show consistent directional shadow; fire sword replaces shadow with ambient glow bloom — inconsistent. (4) SHADOW COUNT: peers use 1 drop-shadow layer; candidate has zero discrete shadow, relying on fire emission — inconsistent. (5) HIGHLIGHT INTENSITY: peers have 1-2 subtle highlight pixels; candidate has a large bright-white highlight cluster at blade tip — significantly over-bright. (6) COLOR TEMPERATURE: peers cluster near cool-neutral with minor warm gold accents; candidate is saturated orange/red/yellow wall-to-wall — the worst offender. (7) DETAIL DENSITY: comparable pixel density to peers — acceptable. (8) PROPORTION: fits peer bounding-box scale — OK. (9) RENDERING STYLE: pixel art consistent — OK. (10) PALETTE OVERLAP: candidate's orange-red-yellow palette shares almost no colors with the steel/gray/dark-brown peer palette; the gold staff is the closest peer but it is desaturated metallic vs vivid flame — low overlap. (11) CHARM LEVEL: peers carry quiet medieval-gear charm; candidate skews toward flashy RPG icon aesthetic — minor misalignment but not fatal. Two hard failures (color temp, palette) plus two softer divergences (highlight, shadow) keep this at marginal rather than pass.
